title: The Price of a Friend
subtitle: Judas goes out to the council and sells his Master for thirty pieces of silver

stillness: Be still, and let the noise of the festival fall away.
A council is gathering in secret; a heart is closing its door.
gospel: Then the chief priests gathered together,
and with them the elders of the people,
into the court of the high priest, who was called Caiaphas;
and they took counsel to take Jesus by deceit, and to put him to death.
But they said: Not on the feast day,
lest perhaps there should be a tumult among the people.
Then Satan entered into Judas, who was surnamed Iscariot,
And he went, and came to the council, and said to them:
What will you give me, and I will deliver him to you?
And they appointed him thirty pieces of silver.
behold: Mark well here: it is the greater ones who chiefly seek to destroy Jesus;
for from the princes and the elders the iniquity went out of Babylon.
See how Satan entered into Judas, that is, into the soul of Judas;
he entered not by forcing, but finding the door standing open;
for Judas, forgetting all that he had seen, turned his gaze to greed alone.
who gave so much for so little:
God for a coin, the true for the empty, the eternal for the passing.
He demanded no fixed sum, as is done for a precious thing,
but, as one handing over a cheap and worthless slave,
he left it to the buyers' will how much they would give.
consider: They who ought to have prepared themselves for the paschal lamb
instead arm themselves against the true Lamb.
In every mortal sin the devil enters a man,
to possess him anew, or more fully than before.
Judas, one of the twelve: in number, not in merit;
in name, not in office; in body, not in soul.
He was not invited by the princes, not compelled, bound by no necessity;
but of his own will he went forth, and of his own malice conceived the counsel of a wicked mind.
A soul greedy of gain does not fear to perish even for a trifle,
and there is no trace of justice in that heart where avarice has made its dwelling.
So many go to their spectacles, and there sell Christ
for a small temptation and a small price;
Judas gave him for thirty pieces, but sinners often give him for less.
Beware, then, of small things; for he who despises the small, little by little falls away.
prayer: who bore the labors of preaching, the weariness of journeying,
the insults and reproaches, the injuries and the anguish, for our salvation,
and at last willed to be sold by one of your own disciples,
and to be priced by the Jews at thirty pieces of silver,
and so to be sold at a worthless price:
grant me to imitate the example of such patience and humility,
and by your grace and your counsel to overcome with patience
the reproaches, injuries, and all things laid upon me,
and never to barter you wickedly away for any passing thing,
and to bear my own contempt and anguish, with even mind, for the glory of your name.

latinJewel.latin: Infelix mercator Judas, qui tantum dedit pro tanto, Deum pro nummo, verum pro vano, æternum pro transitorio!
latinJewel.english: Wretched merchant Judas, who gave so much for so little: God for a coin, the true for the empty, the eternal for the passing!
